ops.title: tilth verdict: per-op peel-off, not replacement
ops.body: Decision (2026-07-13, plan-approved): ccx keeps tilth for symbol/deps/diff/read/overview/outline-fallback and replaces ops natively only where tilth is broken and decoupled. Rationale: OpFind was Finalize-passthrough (no render-grammar coupling) and broken four ways (`.jj` walk leak, nondeterministic MAX_FILES=20 race, bytes/4 estimates on binaries, budget-proof cap) with no flag-level workaround (no exclude/negation flags; `.tilthignore` rejected upstream 2026-07-03; `--budget` never lifts the cap) — replaced by `internal/find` in v0.15.0. Symbol/Deps have no substitute (cross-language zero-build call graph; difftastic is human-output-only; ctags is defs-only/GPL); Diff feeds two independent regex parsers (render/diff.go + cli/history.go symbolHeader). Upstream (jahala/tilth) is alive and merges external PRs but its release train stalls for weeks — narrow fixes filed as PRs #184-187 + issue #188 (grok misses ALL Go type/const/var declarations — only funcs/methods resolve). Codex architecture review concurred (95%). Escape-hatch deviation from the original plan text: an anchored glob bypasses ignore FILES but keeps VCS stores excluded unless the anchor names one — the literal "bypasses everything" reading leaked `.git/HEAD` on `./**/*`. Future fork trigger (codex): if grammar drift keeps breaking the regex lanes, the play is a structured-JSON output API for symbol/deps/diff upstream, not a wholesale replacement.
